title: Intricate Crisp Turkish Mung Bean Sprout Pilaf
description: A grassy and crisp Turkish dish featuring mung bean sprouts, this pilaf is a show-off recipe that is FODMAP friendly. It is made with blanched mung bean sprouts and various spices.
instructions: Rinse the mung bean sprouts and blanch them in boiling water for 30 seconds.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Add the turmeric, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the blanched mung bean sprouts to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the cooked basmati rice to the skillet and stir to combine.
Cook the pilaf for 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until it is crispy and golden brown.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
